description: Phase 1A: Fix the project reference / build ordering issue that causes TS6305 errors across all dependent packages. Add build scripts to leaf packages that lack them (common, auth, ai, realtime, shared-ui, etc.), add 'prisma generate' as a prerequisite for database typecheck, and update turbo.json so typecheck depends on ^build (already set up for ^typecheck but packages don't produce dist/ output). This unblocks typecheck for all packages that currently fail with 'Output file has not been built from source file'.
steps: Add 'build': 'tsc' script to packages that have typecheck but no build script AND are referenced by other packages via tsconfig references: common, auth, ai, realtime, shared-ui, agent-runtime, api-client, federation, ml-pipeline, ml-runtime, moderation, ranking, recommendations, security, server-core, sync-engine, testing. (Some of these already have build scripts - only add where missing.)

For packages/database/package.json: keep the existing prisma generate build script. Add a 'prebuild' or modify the typecheck script to run prisma generate first, OR add a 'generate' script and make typecheck depend on it. The simplest fix: change typecheck to 'prisma generate && tsc --noEmit'.

Update turbo.json: change typecheck task to depend on '^build' instead of '^typecheck'. This ensures that when @quant/auth typechecks, @quant/common's build (tsc producing dist/) runs first. The current config has 'dependsOn: ["^typecheck"]' but typecheck uses --noEmit which doesn't produce dist/.
For packages that reference @quant/auth (server-core), ensure auth is in their build dependency chain by having the turbo topological sort handle it via ^build.
Verify: run 'pnpm typecheck' and confirm packages that previously failed with TS6305 now either pass or show only their own local errors (not missing dist from dependencies).
acceptance_criteria: No TS6305 errors ('Output file has not been built from source file') in any package during pnpm typecheck
@quant/database typecheck no longer fails with missing Prisma client types (AISession, Email, etc.)
All packages that need to be built as dependencies have a 'build' script in their package.json
Turbo correctly builds dependencies before typechecking dependents
verification: Run 'pnpm typecheck 2>&1 | grep TS6305' and confirm zero results
Run 'pnpm --filter @quant/auth run typecheck' and confirm it passes
Run 'pnpm --filter @quant/server-core run typecheck' and confirm no TS6305 errors
findings: turbo typecheck with ^build dependency works correctly - zero TS6305 errors. @quant/common builds cleanly producing dist/. @quant/auth and @quant/server-core typecheck pass through turbo. Database typecheck runs prisma generate successfully but has pre-existing local TS errors (TS2344, TS6133 in test files). lint-staged pre-commit hook ran prettier on all committed files. Packages that have their own TS errors (ai, realtime, etc.) will fail their own build but that does not block downstream packages from building their own clean dependencies.
